summary: Matched sysdolphin/baselib/hash.c fully enough to remove the old hash.s assembly object from the build. The PR adds a C implementation of HashSearchEntry, adjusts HSD_HashSearch signatures and hash class-info typing, updates class.c with an explicit cast at the callsite, switches configure.py and obj_files.mk from asm/hash.s to src/hash.c, and updates symbols for newly emitted local strings and neighboring data labels.
what_changed: Deleted asm/sysdolphin/baselib/hash.s, removing the handwritten assembly for HashSearchEntry and HSD_HashSearch.
Added a C implementation of HashSearchEntry in src/sysdolphin/baselib/hash.c that searches a hash table bucket and optionally writes back a pointer-related result through the ptr parameter.

Changed HSD_HashSearch to use parameter name hash instead of hashp, compute idx through hash->parent.class_info->getidx(hash), assert idx < hash->table_size at line 113, call HashSearchEntry, set *success to !!entry, and return entry->value when found.
Changed HSD_HashSearch return type from HSD_ClassInfo* to HSD_HashClassInfo* in hash.c and hash.h.

Updated hash.h so HSD_HashClass::class_info points to struct _HSD_HashClassInfo instead of struct _HSD_HashInfo, renamed the struct tag to _HSD_HashClassInfo, and added a keycheck callback member with signature bool (*keycheck)(HSD_Hash* hash, void* table_key, void* key).
Updated hsdSearchClassInfo in class.c to cast HSD_HashSearch(current_hash, (void*) class_name, 0) back to HSD_ClassInfo*.
Marked sysdolphin/baselib/hash.c as Matching in configure.py.
Changed obj_files.mk to build src/sysdolphin/baselib/hash.c.o instead of asm/sysdolphin/baselib/hash.s.o.
Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt for hash.c local assert strings at 0x80407720 and 0x804D5FC0, and also corrected nearby displayfunc data/sdata labels into zOne, yOne, zTwo, and local string labels.
smart_moves: The PR did the full decomp handoff cleanly: delete the asm file, switch obj_files.mk to the C object, and mark the source as Matching in configure.py in the same change.
HashSearchEntry was reconstructed with two loop forms matching the assembly behavior: a pointer-to-pointer traversal when ptr is non-NULL, and a direct HSD_HashEntry* traversal when ptr is NULL.

The implementation preserves the assembly-observed virtual callback access pattern through hash->parent.class_info->getidx and hash->parent.class_info->keycheck, which required adding keycheck to HSD_HashClassInfo in the header.
The HSD_ASSERT source strings were allowed to become normal local compiler-emitted symbols rather than keeping artificial global HSD_Hash_80407720 and HSD_Hash_804D5FC0 names.

The callsite in class.c was adjusted with an explicit cast instead of forcing HSD_HashSearch to keep the previous HSD_ClassInfo* return type, suggesting the local hash API typing was corrected for matching even though callers may still expect class-info-like values.
decomp_lessons: When matching a baselib object, update the build graph and matching status together: obj_files.mk must stop referencing the asm object, and configure.py must classify the C source as Matching.

Header accuracy can be the key to matching source: this PR needed the HSD_HashClassInfo struct tag/name, class_info pointer type, and keycheck function pointer to match how the assembly called through offsets 0x3c and 0x40.
Pointer-to-pointer list traversal can be necessary for matching functions that optionally return a link location rather than just a found node; HashSearchEntry’s ptr path mirrors this pattern.
Symbol table cleanup is part of finishing a match: once data comes from C, artificial global labels for assert strings may need to become local @NN labels with correct sizes and scopes.
If an API’s decompiled return type is refined, existing callers may need explicit casts to preserve source intent and compile cleanly.
naming_conventions: Kept exported baselib function names in HSD style: HashSearchEntry and HSD_HashSearch.
Used HSD_HashClassInfo as the canonical typedef and struct tag base instead of the older _HSD_HashInfo tag.
Used lower-case local variable names hash, key, success, entry, and idx in hash.c.
Symbols for compiler-generated strings were represented as local @NN entries in config/GALE01/symbols.txt rather than global HSD_Hash_8040xxxx names.
Nearby display function constants were renamed to semantic names zOne, yOne, and zTwo where the symbol update indicated known data.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced the 151-line assembly implementation of hash.s with C and verified it was suitable to build as src/sysdolphin/baselib/hash.c.o.
Used `if (hash->table[idx] == NULL) return NULL;` before the loop, matching the early null-bucket return visible in the deleted assembly.
Used `!!entry` to generate the boolean success value from the found entry, corresponding to the assembly’s nonzero-to-1 conversion before storing through success.
Preserved assertion line number 113 for `idx < hash->table_size`, matching the deleted assembly’s `li r4, 0x71` before `__assert`.
Split ptr and non-ptr paths in HashSearchEntry rather than trying to share one loop, likely because the generated code shape needed to match the original control flow.
Updated symbols for assert strings `idx < hash->table_size` and `hash.c` to local labels, aligning emitted C data with the final binary layout.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context, so there is no direct reviewer feedback to extract.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that modify HSD_HashSearch return type or HSD_HashClassInfo to see whether the HSD_HashClassInfo* return was retained.
Search for other HashSearchEntry callers with a non-NULL ptr argument to understand the intended pointer-to-pointer writeback semantics.
Audit neighboring displayfunc symbol changes around zOne, yOne, and zTwo to see if they were incidental symbol cleanup or required by hash.c data layout.
Look for additional sysdolphin/baselib objects converted from asm to C using the same configure.py and obj_files.mk handoff pattern.
